body.body:
From D115 (closed as ticketed). Akien 2026-04-14 thought this was done; checked the live DB and found the conceptual pieces are missing.

## Current state

- **memory_blobs table:** exists in Postgres, 251 blobs, 792kb total. Accessed by id only, never scanned. ✓ this part works.
- **Blob facia memories:** ZERO (`SELECT count(*) FROM memories WHERE facia_role='blob_index'` → 0). Blobs are just rows; nothing in the matrix points at them.
- **Blob index tree:** ZERO (no trees with name or description matching blob).
- **wg_cooccur split to LMDB/RocksDB** (also part of D115): NOT done, still in Postgres. Skipping this part — looks like premature optimization at current scale (8 wg_* tables, fine where they are).

## What needs building

### 1. Per-blob facia memories
When a blob is stored (via cortex.store_blob or wherever the write path is), also create a REFERENCE memory with metadata.facia_role='blob_index' that:
- Carries a pointer back to the memory_blobs row (memory_id or blob_id)
- Has a narrative summarizing what's in the blob (title + author + first paragraph for ebooks; topic + source for web content; etc.)
- Has metadata for retrieval: content_type, source, length, tags, created_at
- Lives under a parent BLOB_ROOT facia (see #2)
- Is searchable like any other memory — Igor can find blobs via cortex.search() rather than via a separate blob query API

This is the biomimicry-honest framing: the matrix knows about every blob it owns, the same way the matrix knows about every relationship and every habit. Blobs become *retrievable as memories*, not as a separate filesystem-style store.

### 2. BLOB_ROOT facia + blob_index tree
New root facia BLOB_ROOT (mirror of PR_ROOT) with all blob facia as children. New entry in the trees table named 'blob_index' with facia_id=BLOB_ROOT, descriptive of the blob graph. Lets Igor walk the blob index as a navigable subtree.

### 3. Migration of existing 251 blobs
Backfill facia memories for the 251 existing blobs. Read each blob, derive a narrative + metadata, create the facia row. Idempotent (skip if facia already exists). One-time pass.

### 4. Wire-in to the read path
cortex.expand_blob_memories() already does relevance≥0.5 expansion (DP9). Update it to walk via blob facia rather than (or in addition to) the current path, so the matrix-native retrieval is the primary route.

## Why this matters

Under the cockpit/COA framing from this morning's discussions:
- Igor needs to *know what he has stored* as part of his preparation work — not via a separate query API, but via the same memory-search machinery he uses for everything else
- The reading pipeline (D334 unified-reading-tool) creates a lot of blobs and the relationship between 'thing I read' and 'memories I formed from reading it' is currently tracked outside the memory graph
- Igor's self-capability awareness (T-self-capability-awareness) is partly 'what knowledge do I have access to?' — which is currently invisible because blobs are off-graph

## Existing work to check

- wild_igor/igor/cognition/blob_store.py — current blob storage path
- wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py — expand_blob_memories(), DP9
- wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py m032/m033 — blob CASCADE migrations
- wild_igor/igor/tools/blobs.py — blob tool surface
- wild_igor/igor/tools/persistent_relationships.py — facia pattern to mirror
- wild_igor/igor/memory/tree_index.py — trees infrastructure (D257)
- wild_igor/igor/tools/seed_persistent_relationships.py — pattern for seeding root facia + tree

## Out of scope

- wg_cooccur → LMDB split (D115's other half — premature optimization at current scale)
- Migrating blob storage off Postgres (memory_blobs table is fine where it is)
- Yesterday's verbatim truncation work (T-verbatim-prompt-injection / T-tool-result-verbatim — that was the surgical fix for the immediate problem and is the right shape; this ticket is the longer-term matrix-native blob retrieval, not a replacement for it)

## Provenance

D115 (Akien 2026-04-11, surfaced 2026-04-14 — Akien thought it was done; live DB check showed the facia + tree pieces missing). Connects to yesterday's truncation-fix work as a longer-term complement, not a replacement.
body.title: Each blob gets a facia memory + a graph tree as the blob index
body.result: ensure_blob_facia: FACTUAL facia (facia_role=blob_index) + interpretive edge + tree index for any blob. Idempotent. 8 tests.
